title: Evas Fuzzing Up My Brain
wikitext:
[]: Eva's Fuzzing Up My Brain             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Oct 20, 2007 8:08 am *Caution: Ahead is a rather random post made up of the wanderings of my brain that seems to be begging me to put all the crap it's holding in print.* A funny thing has been happening to me lately; as I'm reading posts (and doing other things) my mind keeps drifting. Eva keeps popping in, but not in the way it used to. Instead of pondering the depths of Eva's narrative, my brain seems to keep turning towards more serious stuff. I've been trying to figure out the cause of this discontent and I can't quite put my finger on it. Ideas and thoughts creep in, but whatever I'm supposed to know seems to remain ephemeral and just out of reach. I'm starting to feel as if I'm grasping at shadows. It's weird... it feels like there's something in there that wants to get out but can't. In an effort to alleviate this mood I've began compiling some documents of various things I've written on Eva in various places. Perhaps I'll be satisfied when I can sort through past thoughts, but I think this inkling is leading me down a different road. I think that something in my head is trying to lead me somewhere using Eva as a road map. Anyway, this post is essentially useless to everyone but me, so lock it if you want. But, just to have something to discuss: anyone else ever get to feeling like this? Perhaps it's the change of seasons... always causes me to weird it up.

[]: Re: Eva's Fuzzing Up My Brain             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sat Oct 20, 2007 8:29 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: But, just to have something to discuss: anyone else ever get to feeling like this? Perhaps it's the change of seasons... always causes me to weird it up.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Sat Oct 20, 2007 9:56 am Eva makes my head hurt sometimes I get WAY to into it

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Oct 20, 2007 11:01 am The only thing I can think of is maybe you've been into it so much that you're starting to lose your interest in it? I think there have been a few weekends in the past where I've taken a breather from that (e.g: Writing or reading Eva fanfiction, figuring out stuff by watching an episode over again, reading ANF/EMF forum posts, etc) and it seemed to help. Now I'm not saying you're totally losing your interest in it or anything, it's just a thought. (I think maybe Reichu might be going through the same thing, but I could be wrong. *shrugs*)

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Oct 20, 2007 11:05 am It's definitely possible to get burned out on it. Lately I, too, have found myself skimming through these threads, not as interested in reading every single word, as I used to be.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sat Oct 20, 2007 11:10 am I think Eva Yojimbo has already subconsiously worked out some kind of personal meaning that's in Eva and now he's trying to figure it out. I don't see anything there that shows he's losing interest in the series, to be honest. BrikHaus wrote: It's definitely possible to get burned out on it. Lately I, too, have found myself skimming through these threads, not as interested in reading every single word, as I used to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 20, 2007 11:18 am CitizenGeek wrote: Just what is the appeal with fanfiction, anyway?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Oct 20, 2007 11:25 am Actually, Tines, there is this one "A/S" fic you recommend which occurs as EoE from Asuka's perspective, although you have the comment that it should have ended at EoE instead of the slightly WAFFY ending. ;) And apparently, I'm one of the few that's in the "decently written fics" category, as many, many people have commented that to me and/or that they're very picky when it comes to what they read. (Though of course, if you dislike Usagi/Mamoru from SM or Shinji/Asuka, then you probably wouldn't enjoy 'em much.) But anyway, 'Jimbo, I think you could just be getting burned out, especially with the stress of the EMforums switching to the FGP one.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Oct 20, 2007 11:48 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: especially with the stress of the EMforums switching to the FGP one.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Oct 20, 2007 11:50 am I've noticed quite a few people acting stressed at least, so I thought maybe it had to do with that. So have you ever had the aforementioned problem, Brik?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Oct 20, 2007 11:54 am I seriously doubt the change made forumgoers start acting strange, no matter how much stupidity has popped up recently. The only real suffers in the change are the images in the old Eva commentary page, many of which have gone poof thanks to broken links.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Oct 20, 2007 12:03 pm I thought I recall--according to the Evacommentary ANF archive at least--that the images for the broken links are around, just not uploaded. Can't say if that's the case for this, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 20, 2007 12:07 pm If there is stressyness on the forums, it's a result of a month's gap and then an unusually heavy influx of newcomers, not all yet acculturated. Sailor Star Dust wrote: Actually, Tines, there is this one "A/S" fic you recommend

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Oct 20, 2007 12:10 pm Ah, so that's what you mean by usual suspects. I see.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Oct 20, 2007 12:18 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: So have you ever had the aforementioned problem, Brik?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Oct 20, 2007 12:28 pm Yeah. I know you at least mentioned about getting burned out by reading too much lately.

[]:             Quote Post by AnnoShinjiAsuka » Sat Oct 20, 2007 12:36 pm I've noticed quite a few people acting stressed at least, so I thought maybe it had to do with that.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Oct 20, 2007 12:36 pm @ Stardust-chan: For me, it is a combination of a number of things. Firstly, school has become much more intense once I entered clinical rotations. I don't have the free time I used to, so when I check the forum and see a million posts, I kind of skim through the ones I don't find all that interesting. Secondly, over-exposure in terms of doing the Concurrency Project, fiddling with this fanfic I've been tinkering on forever, and trying to stay active in the forum, has made me feel a sense of burning out. Thirdly, it feels like everything that could be discussed has been discussed. I know that's not really the case, but some times it feels that way. I have experienced this before, and rewatching Eva revitalized my interest in it. Perhaps that is in order, but for the moment it will have to wait, because I have too many things on my plate. @ ASA: What do you want to know?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Oct 20, 2007 12:41 pm Brik-kun wrote: it feels like everything that could be discussed has been discussed. I know that's not really the case, but some times it feels that way. I have experienced this before, and rewatching Eva revitalized my interest in it.

[]:             Quote Post by AnnoShinjiAsuka » Sat Oct 20, 2007 1:33 pm @Brikhaus-san: I am simply figuring why would people here get stressed for minor changes. @Sailor Star Dust-san: :) Thank you! Mr. Tines wrote: If there is stressyness on the forums, it's a result of a month's gap and then an unusually heavy influx of newcomers, not all yet acculturated.
